2605.02396.title: HeavySkill: Heavy Thinking as the Inner Skill in Agentic Harness
2605.02396.paper_summary: Recent advances in agentic harness with orchestration frameworks that coordinate multiple agents with memory, skills, and tool use have achieved remarkable success in complex reasoning tasks. However, the underlying mechanism that truly drives performance remains obscured behind intricate system designs. In this paper, we propose HeavySkill, a perspective that views heavy thinking not only as a minimal execution unit in orchestration harness but also as an inner skill internalized within the model's parameters that drives the orchestrator to solve complex tasks. We identify this skill as a two-stage pipeline, i.e., parallel reasoning then summarization, which can operate beneath any agentic harness. We present a systematic empirical study of HeavySkill across diverse domains. Our results show that this inner skill consistently outperforms traditional Best-of-N (BoN) strategies; notably, stronger LLMs can even approach Pass@N performance. Crucially, we demonstrate that the depth and width of heavy thinking, as a learnable skill, can be further scaled via reinforcement learning, offering a promising path toward self-evolving LLMs that internalize complex reasoning without relying on brittle orchestration layers.
2605.02396.paper_summary_zh: 最近在代理式工具與協調框架方面的進展，使得能夠協調多個具備記憶、技能和工具使用的代理，已在複雜推理任務中取得了顯著的成功。<lb/>然而，真正驅動性能的基本機制仍然隱藏在複雜的系統設計背後。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了HeavySkill，一種觀點將重度思考視為不僅僅是協調工具中的最小執行單元，而是作為內部技能，內化於模型的參數中，驅動協調者解決複雜任務。<lb/>我們將這一技能識別為一個兩階段的管道，即平行推理然後總結，這可以在任何代理式工具下運作。<lb/>我們對HeavySkill在不同領域進行了系統的實證研究。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，這一內部技能始終超越傳統的最佳選擇（BoN）策略；值得注意的是，較強的LLM甚至可以接近Pass@N的表現。<lb/>關鍵是，我們證明重度思考的深度和寬度，作為一種可學習的技能，可以通過強化學習進一步擴展，為自我演化的LLM提供了一條有前景的道路，使其能夠內化複雜推理，而無需依賴脆弱的協調層。
2605.02396.author: Jianing Wang et.al.
2605.02396.authors: Jianing Wang, Linsen Guo, Zhengyu Chen, Qi Guo, Hongyu Zang, Wenjie Shi, Haoxiang Ma, Xiangyu Xi, Xiaoyu Li, Wei Wang, Xunliang Cai
